billing_and_payments.overview: CPS Energy offers multiple convenient ways to pay your bill. Most methods are available 24/7.

payment_methods.description: Pay through your CPS Energy My Account portal at cpsenergy.com. You can make one-time payments or set up AutoPay.
Enroll in automatic monthly payments through My Account. Your bill is paid automatically each month from your bank account or card on file.
payment_methods.enrollment: Sign up through My Account portal or call customer service

payment_methods.description: Call the automated payment line to pay by phone using your account number.
Speak with a customer service representative to make a payment over the phone.
payment_methods.availability: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Saturday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

payment_methods.description: Send your payment by mail using the return envelope included with your bill statement.
payment_methods.mailing_address: CPS Energy, P.O. Box 101, San Antonio, TX 78291-0101
payment_methods.note: Allow 5-7 business days for mail delivery. Do not send cash.

payment_methods.method: Pay In Person - CPS Energy Walk-In Centers
payment_methods.description: Visit a CPS Energy customer service center to pay in person.

locations.name: CPS Energy Southeast
locations.address: 3495 E. Houston St, San Antonio, TX 78219

payment_methods.description: Pay at authorized third-party pay station locations throughout San Antonio including select grocery stores, convenience stores, and check cashing locations.
payment_methods.note: Find locations at cpsenergy.com or call 210-353-2222. Payments may take 1-2 business days to post.

payment_methods.description: Use the secure payment drop boxes located at CPS Energy service centers. Available for after-hours payments.
payment_methods.note: Do not deposit cash. Include your account number on your check or money order.

payment_methods.description: Set up CPS Energy as a payee through your bank's online bill pay service.
payment_methods.fee: No fee from CPS Energy (check with your bank)
payment_methods.note: Allow 3-5 business days for electronic payments or 5-7 business days for mailed checks from your bank.

average_billing.name: Average Billing (Level Pay)
average_billing.description: Smooths out seasonal bill fluctuations by averaging your monthly payments over a rolling 12-month period. Your monthly amount is recalculated periodically based on actual usage.
average_billing.eligibility: Account must be in good standing with 12 months of billing history at the service address.
average_billing.enrollment: Enroll through My Account online or call 210-353-2222.

paperless_billing.name: Paperless Billing
paperless_billing.description: Receive your bill electronically via email instead of a paper bill. View and download your bill through My Account.

budget_billing.name: Budget Billing
budget_billing.description: Similar to average billing, helps you plan monthly expenses by stabilizing your bill amount.

bill_components: Base/customer charge - fixed monthly service fee
Energy charge - based on kWh of electricity and/or CCF of natural gas used
Fuel adjustment charge - reflects changes in fuel costs
Regulatory charge - costs associated with regulatory compliance
City of San Antonio franchise fee
understanding_your_bill.meter_reading: Meters are read monthly. If a meter cannot be read, the bill may be estimated based on historical usage.
understanding_your_bill.billing_cycle: Bills are generated monthly. Due date is typically 16 days after the bill date.
understanding_your_bill.late_payment: A late payment penalty of 5% may be applied if payment is not received by the due date.

assistance_programs.overview: CPS Energy and its community partners offer multiple assistance programs to help customers who are struggling to pay their energy bills.

programs.name: Residential Energy Assistance Partnership (REAP)
programs.description: CPS Energy's primary bill assistance program funded by voluntary customer contributions and a one-time $6 million CPS Energy contribution. Provides direct assistance with energy bills for qualifying low-income customers in the CPS Energy service area.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy residential customer
Household income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level
Must be a Bexar County resident for maximum benefit
Must provide proof of income for all household members
Must provide valid government-issued photo ID
Assistance available up to twice per calendar year
programs.how_to_apply: Apply online through the City of San Antonio, call Bexar County at 210-335-3666, call CPS Energy Customer Engagement at 210-353-4483, or dial United Way at 211. You can also use the CPS Energy Assistance Finder tool online to apply for REAP and other programs in a single application.
programs.assistance_amount: Up to $1,200 for Bexar County residents depending on funding availability and need

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.description: Federally funded program administered through the Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs. Helps low-income households with energy costs.
eligibility: Household income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level
Must be responsible for home energy costs
Priority given to elderly, disabled, and families with young children
Must provide Social Security numbers for all household members
programs.how_to_apply: Apply through local Community Action Agency or call 2-1-1 for referral to nearest intake center.
programs.assistance_amount: Varies based on funding and household need

programs.name: CEAP (Comprehensive Energy Assistance Program)
programs.description: State-funded program that provides utility payment assistance and energy education to low-income Texas households.
eligibility: Household income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level
Must be a Texas resident
Must provide proof of income, residency, and identification
programs.how_to_apply: Contact local CEAP sub-recipient agency. In San Antonio, contact the City of San Antonio Department of Human Services.

programs.name: Affordability Discount Program (ADP)
programs.description: CPS Energy discount program that provides a monthly discount on both electricity and natural gas service to qualifying low-income customers.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy residential customer
Household income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level

Must also meet at least one of the following: age 60 or older, have a disability, require life-sustaining medical equipment, have preschool or school-age children in the household, meet veterans discount qualifications, or demonstrate extenuating circumstances
Must apply and be approved annually
programs.how_to_apply: Apply through CPS Energy, a partner agency, or use the Assistance Finder tool at cpsenergy.com. Call 210-353-2222 for details.
programs.benefit: Monthly discount applied directly to your CPS Energy bill for both electric and gas service

programs.name: Project WARM (Weatherization and Appliance Replacement Modification)
programs.description: Free home weatherization program that helps reduce energy costs by improving home energy efficiency. Includes insulation, weather-stripping, caulking, and HVAC repairs or replacements.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy residential customer
Household income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level
Must own or have landlord permission for weatherization work
Home must be in the CPS Energy service area
programs.how_to_apply: Call CPS Energy at 210-353-4670 or apply online at cpsenergy.com.
programs.benefit: Free home weatherization improvements valued at several thousand dollars

programs.name: Medical Protection Program
programs.description: Provides protection from disconnection for customers or household members who have a serious medical condition that requires electric-powered life support equipment.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy residential customer
Requires a physician's certification that a household member requires electric-powered life support equipment
Must be renewed every 90 days
programs.how_to_apply: Complete the Medical Protection form and submit a physician's certification. Call 210-353-2222 for the form.
programs.benefit: Protection from disconnection for medical necessity (does not eliminate the obligation to pay)

programs.name: Payment Arrangements / Deferred Payment Plans
programs.description: CPS Energy offers flexible payment arrangements for customers who are behind on their bills. Allows you to spread past-due amounts over multiple installments.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy customer with a past-due balance
Account must not have a broken payment arrangement in the last 12 months
programs.how_to_apply: Call 210-353-2222 or visit a CPS Energy walk-in center to set up a payment plan.
programs.benefit: Spread past-due balance over 3-6 monthly installments while keeping service connected

programs.name: Military and Veteran Assistance
programs.description: Special protections and assistance for active military members and veterans, including protection from disconnection during active deployment.
eligibility: Active duty military personnel
Veterans in need of bill assistance
Must provide proof of military status
programs.how_to_apply: Call CPS Energy at 210-353-2222 and identify yourself as military/veteran. You may also be referred to partner organizations like the Salvation Army and Catholic Charities.
programs.benefit: Disconnection protection during deployment, referral to military-specific assistance programs

programs.name: Salvation Army Assistance
programs.description: The Salvation Army in San Antonio provides emergency utility assistance to qualifying families in crisis.
eligibility: Must demonstrate financial hardship
Must provide proof of income and CPS Energy bill
Assistance available based on funding
programs.how_to_apply: Contact the Salvation Army San Antonio at 210-352-2000 or visit a local office.

programs.name: Catholic Charities Utility Assistance
programs.description: Catholic Charities of San Antonio provides emergency utility bill assistance to families in need.
eligibility: Must demonstrate financial hardship
Must provide required documentation
Bexar County residents
programs.how_to_apply: Call Catholic Charities at 210-222-1294 to schedule an appointment.

programs.name: City of San Antonio Utility Assistance
programs.description: The City of San Antonio Department of Human Services provides utility assistance programs for qualifying residents.
eligibility: Must be a San Antonio / Bexar County resident
Household income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level
Must provide required documentation
programs.how_to_apply: Contact the City of San Antonio Department of Human Services at 210-207-8198 or call 3-1-1.

programs.name: 211 Texas / United Way Referral Service
programs.description: Dial 2-1-1 for a free referral service that connects you to local assistance programs for utilities, food, housing, and other needs.
eligibility: Available to all Texas residents
programs.how_to_apply: Dial 2-1-1 from any phone, or visit 211texas.org.

programs.name: Casa Verde Weatherization Program
programs.description: FREE home weatherization program for qualifying CPS Energy customers. Includes insulation, weather stripping, and energy-efficient lighting upgrades to reduce energy consumption and lower bills. Administered by Franklin Energy Services on behalf of CPS Energy.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy residential customer
Available to qualifying homeowners and renters
Income-qualified (check current thresholds with CPS Energy)
Home must be in the CPS Energy service area
programs.how_to_apply: Apply online using the CPS Energy Assistance Finder tool at cpsenergy.com, or call CPS Energy at 210-353-2222.
programs.benefit: Free home weatherization improvements including insulation, weather stripping, and energy-efficient lighting at no cost to the customer

programs.name: WRAP (Weatherization Repair Assistance Program)
programs.description: Bridges the gap for CPS Energy customers who do not qualify for the Casa Verde Weatherization Program but still need help with home weatherization and energy efficiency improvements.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy residential customer
Customers who do not qualify for Casa Verde but still need weatherization assistance
Income thresholds differ from Casa Verde — check with CPS Energy for current requirements
programs.how_to_apply: Call CPS Energy at 210-353-2222 or apply through the Assistance Finder tool at cpsenergy.com.
programs.benefit: Home weatherization and repair assistance for customers who fall outside Casa Verde eligibility

programs.name: Senior Late Fee Waiver
programs.description: Qualifying senior customers can receive a waiver of late payment fees on their CPS Energy bill.
eligibility: Must be a CPS Energy residential customer
Must be age 60 or older
Must meet income eligibility requirements
programs.how_to_apply: Apply through the CPS Energy Assistance Finder tool at cpsenergy.com or call 210-353-2222.
programs.benefit: Waiver of late payment penalties on your CPS Energy bill

assistance_finder_tool.name: CPS Energy Assistance Finder Tool
assistance_finder_tool.description: Launched in July 2024, the Assistance Finder is a single online application that screens customers for eligibility across multiple assistance programs at once. Nearly 29,000 customers have used the tool since launch.
assistance_finder_tool.how_to_use: Visit cpsenergy.com and navigate to the Assistance Finder tool. Complete one application and the system will determine which programs you may qualify for.

outage_reporting.overview: CPS Energy works to restore power as quickly and safely as possible during outages. You can report outages and check restoration status through multiple channels.

report_methods.description: Call to report an outage, downed power line, or check outage status. Available 24/7.
report_methods.note: This is the emergency and outage line.

report_methods.description: View real-time outage information on the interactive outage map. See affected areas, estimated restoration times, and number of customers impacted.
Report an outage and check status through your CPS Energy My Account portal online.
Report outages and track restoration status through the CPS Energy mobile app, available on iOS and Android.
Text OUT to 27000 to report an outage. You must first register your mobile number with your CPS Energy account.
Follow @CPSEnergy on Twitter/X and Facebook for outage updates and restoration information during major events.

outage_safety_tips: Stay away from downed power lines - always assume they are energized
Report downed lines immediately by calling 210-353-4357
Turn off major appliances to prevent a power surge when electricity is restored
Keep refrigerator and freezer doors closed to preserve food (refrigerator keeps food cold ~4 hours, freezer ~48 hours if full)
Use flashlights instead of candles to prevent fire hazards
Never use generators, grills, or camp stoves indoors due to carbon monoxide risk
Check on elderly and vulnerable neighbors during extended outages
If you rely on electric-powered medical equipment, have a backup plan

restoration_process.description: CPS Energy follows a systematic restoration process to restore power to the greatest number of customers as safely and quickly as possible.
steps: Assess damage and identify cause of outage
Repair transmission lines and substations first (restores power to largest groups)
Repair individual tap lines serving neighborhoods
Repair individual service lines to homes and businesses
restoration_process.note: Restoration times vary based on the cause and extent of damage. During major storms, restoration may take longer.

start_service.description: Start new electric and/or gas service with CPS Energy.

methods.description: Start service online through the CPS Energy website. This is the fastest method.
Call customer service to start service.
Visit a CPS Energy walk-in center with required documents.

requirements: Valid government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, state ID, or military ID)
Social Security Number or ITIN
Service address and move-in date
Mailing address (if different from service address)
May require a deposit based on credit check or previous CPS Energy history

deposit_info.description: A security deposit may be required based on creditworthiness or payment history.
waiver_options: Letter of credit from a previous utility company
Previous CPS Energy account in good standing
deposit_info.refund: Deposits are refunded after 12 months of on-time payments or when the account is closed.

start_service.timeline: Request service at least 2-3 business days before your desired start date. Same-day connections may be available but are not guaranteed.

stop_service.description: Disconnect your CPS Energy service when moving out.

methods.description: Call customer service to stop service.

requirements: Provide your account number or service address
Provide the date you want service disconnected
Provide a forwarding/mailing address for your final bill
Request stop at least 2 business days in advance
stop_service.final_bill: A final bill will be mailed to your forwarding address. Any remaining balance must be paid, and any deposit (plus interest) will be applied to the final bill. If a credit remains, a refund check will be issued.
stop_service.note: You are responsible for all energy usage at the service address until the disconnection date.

transfer_service.description: Transfer your CPS Energy service from one address to another within the CPS Energy service area.

methods.description: Call customer service to transfer service.

requirements: Current account information
New service address and move-in date
Date to disconnect at current address
Account must be in good standing
transfer_service.timeline: Request transfer at least 3 business days before your move date.
transfer_service.note: Your account number will remain the same. A final bill for the old address and a new bill for the new address will be generated.

reconnect_service.description: Reconnect electric service that was disconnected for non-payment.

methods.description: Use the online self-service reconnection form through My Account to request reconnection after paying the required amount.
Call customer service to arrange reconnection and payment.
Visit a CPS Energy walk-in center to pay and request reconnection.

requirements: Pay the past-due amount or arrange a payment plan
May require a reconnection fee
Outstanding balance must be resolved before reconnection
reconnect_service.note: Online self-service reconnection is available for electric service disconnected for non-payment. Reconnection times vary.

energy_savings.overview: CPS Energy offers numerous programs and rebates to help customers save energy and money while reducing their environmental footprint.

step_program.name: Save for Tomorrow Energy Plan (STEP)
step_program.description: CPS Energy's comprehensive energy efficiency program that offers rebates and incentives for energy-efficient upgrades to your home or business.

residential_rebates.description: Rebate for installing a high-efficiency central air conditioning system.
residential_rebates.rebate: Varies by efficiency rating — check resi-savenow.cpsenergy.com for current amounts

residential_rebates.description: Rebate for installing a qualifying heat pump system.
residential_rebates.rebate: Varies by efficiency rating — check resi-savenow.cpsenergy.com for current amounts

residential_rebates.description: Rebate for purchasing a qualifying energy-efficient window air conditioning unit.
residential_rebates.rebate: Varies — check resi-savenow.cpsenergy.com for current amounts

residential_rebates.description: Rebate for adding or upgrading attic insulation.
Rebate for installing a qualifying cool/reflective roof.
Rebate for installing a variable-speed or energy-efficient pool pump.
Rebate for installing a qualifying ductless mini-split heat pump system.
FREE professional home energy assessment to identify where your home is losing energy and recommend improvements.
FREE professional tune-up of your central air conditioning system to optimize performance and efficiency.

residential_rebates.item: LED Light Bulbs and Air Purifiers
residential_rebates.description: Instant discounts on LED light bulbs and air purifiers at participating retail stores in the CPS Energy service area.

step_program.how_to_apply: Submit rebate applications online at resi-savenow.cpsenergy.com or email savenow@cpsenergy.com. Pre-approval may be required for some rebates. Keep all receipts and contractor invoices.

nest_thermostat_program.name: Smart Thermostat / Nest Program
nest_thermostat_program.description: CPS Energy partners with smart thermostat manufacturers to offer discounts on smart thermostats. Customers may also participate in demand response programs where CPS Energy can make minor temperature adjustments during peak demand events in exchange for bill credits.
benefits: Discounted or free smart thermostat (when available)
Average energy savings of 10-15% on heating and cooling costs
Bill credits for participating in demand response / Rush Hour Rewards events
Remote control of your thermostat via smartphone
nest_thermostat_program.how_to_enroll: Visit cpsenergy.com or call 210-353-2222 to check current thermostat offers and demand response program availability.

ev_program.name: Electric Vehicle (EV) Program
ev_program.description: CPS Energy supports the growing adoption of electric vehicles with special programs and resources for EV owners.
offerings: Information on EV charging station installation
Time-of-use rate options that offer lower rates for overnight EV charging
Resources for commercial EV fleet charging
Public charging station locations in the CPS Energy service area
ev_charging_tips: Charge your EV during off-peak hours (typically 9 PM - 6 AM) for lowest rates
Consider a Level 2 (240V) home charger for faster charging
A licensed electrician should install your home charging equipment
ev_program.contact: Call 210-353-2222 for more information about EV programs and rates.

solar_program.name: Solar / Distributed Generation Program
solar_program.description: CPS Energy supports customers who want to install solar panels or other renewable energy systems on their property.

options.name: Rooftop Solar (Customer-Owned)
options.description: Install your own solar panel system. CPS Energy offers a solar buyback program where excess energy generated is credited to your account.

options.name: SolarHost Program
options.description: CPS Energy's community solar program. Solar panels are installed on your roof at no cost to you, and you receive a monthly bill credit.

options.name: Solar Buyback / Net Metering
options.description: Customers with solar panels can sell excess electricity back to CPS Energy. Credits are applied to your monthly bill based on the current buyback rate.

requirements: System must be installed by a licensed contractor
Must submit an interconnection agreement to CPS Energy before installation
System must pass inspection and meet CPS Energy technical requirements
Net metering available for systems up to 25 kW (residential)
solar_program.contact: Call CPS Energy at 210-353-2222 or visit cpsenergy.com for current solar program details and buyback rates.

demand_response_program.name: Demand Response Program
demand_response_program.description: Earn financial incentives for conserving energy during peak usage periods. When conservation events are called, CPS Energy may make minor thermostat adjustments (slightly higher) for participating customers with connected smart thermostats to help reduce strain on the grid.
benefits: Financial incentives and bill credits for participation
Helps prevent rolling blackouts during extreme heat or cold
Thermostat adjustments are minor and temporary
Customers can opt out of individual events
demand_response_program.how_to_enroll: Enroll through your smart thermostat program or call CPS Energy at 210-353-2222.

commercial_programs.name: Commercial Energy Efficiency Programs
commercial_programs.description: CPS Energy offers energy efficiency programs and rebates for commercial and industrial customers.
offerings: Lighting retrofits and LED upgrade rebates
commercial_programs.contact: Call the commercial line at 210-353-3333 for details.

my_energy_portal.name: My Energy Portal
my_energy_portal.description: Online tool that helps customers track their energy usage over time, set energy-saving goals, and receive personalized tips to reduce consumption and lower bills.
my_energy_portal.access: Log in to My Account at cpsenergy.com to access the My Energy Portal.

energy_cost_calculator.name: Energy Cost Calculator
energy_cost_calculator.description: Online tool that allows customers to estimate the cost of running individual appliances based on wattage and usage hours. Helps identify which appliances use the most energy.

energy_saving_tips: Set your thermostat to 78°F in summer and 68°F in winter
Use ceiling fans to supplement your AC (counterclockwise in summer)
Seal air leaks around windows, doors, and ductwork
Use LED light bulbs which use 75% less energy than incandescent
Unplug electronics and chargers when not in use (phantom load)
Run dishwashers and washing machines with full loads
Use a programmable or smart thermostat
Close blinds and curtains during the hottest part of the day
Ensure your attic has adequate insulation (R-38 or higher recommended for San Antonio)

contact_info.overview: CPS Energy provides multiple ways to reach customer service for different needs.

residential_customer_service.description: Residential customer service for billing, payments, account inquiries, start/stop/transfer service, and general questions.
residential_customer_service.hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Saturday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM
residential_customer_service.after_hours: Automated system available 24/7 for payments, outage reporting, and account balance.

commercial_customer_service.description: Commercial and industrial customer service for business accounts.

emergency_and_outage.description: Report power outages, gas leaks, downed power lines, and other emergencies. Available 24/7.
emergency_and_outage.note: For life-threatening emergencies, always call 911 first.

street_light_outage.description: Report street light outages. You can also report them online at cpsenergy.com.

energy_efficiency_programs.description: Questions about rebates, energy efficiency programs, Project WARM, and STEP.

call_before_you_dig.description: Call 811 before any digging project to have underground utility lines marked for free. Required by Texas law.

online_resources.mobile_app: Available on iOS App Store and Google Play Store (search 'CPS Energy')

walk_in_centers.name: CPS Energy Main Plaza (Headquarters)
walk_in_centers.hours: Monday-Friday 7:30 AM - 5:30 PM (extended to 6:45 PM on Wednesdays)

walk_in_centers.name: CPS Energy West
walk_in_centers.hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (extended to 6:45 PM on Wednesdays)

walk_in_centers.name: CPS Energy Southeast
walk_in_centers.address: 3495 E. Houston St, San Antonio, TX 78219
walk_in_centers.hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (extended to 6:45 PM on Wednesdays)

contact_info.night_deposit_boxes: Night deposit boxes are available 24/7 at all CPS Energy service centers for after-hours check and money order payments.

mailing_address.payments: CPS Energy, P.O. Box 101, San Antonio, TX 78291-0101
mailing_address.general_correspondence: CPS Energy, 145 Navarro St, San Antonio, TX 78205

contact_info.service_area: CPS Energy serves the greater San Antonio, Texas metropolitan area in Bexar County and portions of surrounding counties. It is the nation's largest municipally-owned electric and gas utility, serving over 950,000 electric customers and more than 389,000 natural gas customers. Established in 1860, CPS Energy bills rank among the lowest of the nation's 20 largest cities. CPS Energy has generated $9.6 billion in revenue for the City of San Antonio over 80 years and is ranked #1 in Texas for solar generation.

safety.overview: CPS Energy is committed to public safety. Learn how to stay safe around electricity and natural gas.

downed_power_lines.description: Downed power lines are extremely dangerous and can be fatal.
tips: Always assume a downed power line is energized and dangerous
Stay at least 35 feet away from downed lines
Do not touch anything in contact with a downed power line (cars, fences, trees)
If your car is in contact with a downed line, stay inside and call 911
Call CPS Energy immediately at 210-353-4357 to report downed lines
If you must exit a vehicle near a downed line, jump clear without touching the vehicle and the ground at the same time, then shuffle away with small steps

outdoor_safety: Keep ladders, antennas, and kites at least 10 feet from power lines
Never climb utility poles or transmission towers
Call 811 before digging to locate underground lines
Keep trees trimmed away from power lines (CPS Energy provides free tree trimming near power lines)
Do not build structures or plant trees under or near power lines
Stay away from substations and electrical equipment
indoor_safety: Do not overload electrical outlets or extension cords
Replace damaged or frayed electrical cords immediately
Use Ground Fault Circuit Interrupters (GFCIs) in wet areas like bathrooms and kitchens
Keep electrical appliances away from water
Install and maintain smoke detectors on every level of your home
Have a licensed electrician perform electrical work
generator_safety: Never operate a generator indoors or in an enclosed space
Place generators at least 20 feet from your home
Never connect a generator directly to home wiring (use a transfer switch installed by a licensed electrician)
Allow generators to cool before refueling
Install carbon monoxide detectors in your home

signs: Rotten egg or sulfur-like smell (added odorant called mercaptan)
Hissing or blowing sound near gas lines or appliances
Dead or dying vegetation in an otherwise green area
Dirt or dust blowing from a hole in the ground

what_to_do_if_you_smell_gas: Leave the area immediately - do not try to find the leak
Do NOT turn on/off any electrical switches, lights, or appliances
Do NOT use your phone inside the building - call from a safe distance
Do NOT light matches, candles, or any open flame
Do NOT start vehicles near the suspected leak
Call CPS Energy at 210-353-4357 from a safe location
Call 911 if you believe there is immediate danger
Do not return until CPS Energy or emergency responders say it is safe
gas_appliance_safety: Have gas appliances inspected annually by a qualified technician
Ensure proper ventilation for all gas appliances
Keep flammable materials away from gas appliances
Install carbon monoxide detectors near gas appliances and sleeping areas
Know how to shut off your gas meter in an emergency
Never use a gas oven or stove to heat your home

carbon_monoxide.description: Carbon monoxide (CO) is a colorless, odorless gas produced by burning fuel. It can be deadly.
prevention: Install CO detectors on every level of your home
Never use generators, grills, or camp stoves indoors
Never run a vehicle in an enclosed garage
carbon_monoxide.emergency: If your CO detector alarms or you suspect CO poisoning, leave immediately, get fresh air, and call 911.

tree_trimming.description: CPS Energy provides free tree trimming services to maintain safe clearances around power lines.
details: CPS Energy trims trees within 10 feet of power lines at no charge
Trimming follows International Society of Arboriculture (ISA) standards
Customers are responsible for trees not near power lines
To request tree trimming near power lines, call 210-353-2222

call_before_you_dig.description: Texas law requires you to call 811 at least 2 business days before any digging project. A locator will mark underground utility lines at no charge.
call_before_you_dig.applies_to: All digging projects including fence posts, landscaping, swimming pools, foundations, and sprinkler systems.
